examples: He must make the proper use of his opportunities or he will infallibly perish.
When the rate of interest rise, such a person's expenditure must almost infallibly be reduced.
If they are real, they will infallibly be discovered, without your pointing them out yourself, and with much more advantage.
This cuts bureaucracy and waste, and can ensure (though not infallibly) that public capital is efficiently allocated among the deserving.
